pi_items.source: Task description (task.json line 5): 'Create a new User Profile in OpenSIS named "Guidance Counselor"'

Task description requires: 'Full Access (Can Use AND Can Edit) to the Students module'. Verified 'students' is a real module in OpenSIS v9.2 (defined in Modules.php and directory openSIS-Classic-9.2/modules/students/ contains Student.php, Search.php, etc.)

Task description requires: 'Read-Only Access (Can Use, but CANNOT Edit) to the Scheduling module'. Verified 'scheduling' is a real module in OpenSIS v9.2 (defined in Modules.php and directory openSIS-Classic-9.2/modules/scheduling/ contains Schedule.php, Search.php, etc.)
install_opensis.sh line 159 specifies OPENSIS_VERSION='V9.2'. Downloaded and verified: README.md states 'Community Edition version 9.2 (Rel date: 27/10/2025)'

pi_items.verified_value: user_profiles (with id, profile, title fields), profile_exceptions (with profile_id, modname, can_use, can_edit fields)
pi_items.source: OpenSIS v9.2 schema file OpensisSchemaMysqlInc.sql verified from downloaded tarball. user_profiles has AUTO_INCREMENT id and varchar(100) title field. profile_exceptions stores varchar(1) can_use and can_edit flags.
Task description (task.json line 5) and setup scripts (setup_opensis.sh, direct_db_setup.sh) all specify username='admin' and password='Admin@123'

pi_items.verified_value: profile_exceptions.modname contains full paths like 'students/Student.php', 'students/Search.php', 'scheduling/Schedule.php', etc. Verifier uses substring matching with keyword 'in modname.lower()' to find all permissions for a module
pi_items.source: export_result.sh extracts modname from profile_exceptions table. verifier.py uses: relevant_perms = [p for p in permissions if keyword in p['modname'].lower()]

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a user profile titled 'Guidance Counselor' in OpenSIS v9.2 with specific module permissions: full read-write access to the Students module and read-only access to the Scheduling module. Both modules are verified to exist in OpenSIS v9.2 (downloaded from GitHub release V9.2). The database schema includes user_profiles table with a title field and profile_exceptions table storing granular module permissions via can_use and can_edit flags. Admin login credentials are admin/Admin@123. Module permissions are stored as full paths (e.g., 'students/Student.php') and verified via substring matching. All metadata values correctly reflect the task requirements and the actual system capabilities.
